title: Remediate: known default cookieSecret passes length guard, usable in production
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review] (cycle 2)
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/util/secrets.ts (lines 27-28, 49-61)

**Finding**:
The hardcoded `defaultSecrets.cookieSecret` value "dev-only-secret-replace-in-production-xx" is exactly 40 characters, so it satisfies the `cookieSecret.length < 32` guard and does NOT trigger a startup failure. If secrets.json is absent (or omits cookieSecret) in production, the app falls back to defaultSecrets and boots with a publicly-known secret. Because this secret now signs better-auth session cookies (replacing cookie-session), an attacker who reads the source can forge a valid admin session cookie and bypass authentication entirely.

**Fix suggestion**:
In src/server/util/secrets.ts, after the existing length check, also reject the known default when in production:
- if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' && secrets.cookieSecret === defaultSecrets.cookieSecret) throw new Error('cookieSecret must not be the built-in default in production');
- OR refuse to fall back to defaultSecrets at all when NODE_ENV === 'production' (require secrets.json to exist).
Do not log the secret value, only the field name.

**Acceptance**:
- Starting the server in production (NODE_ENV=production) with a missing/unset/default cookieSecret throws at startup and does not boot.
- A secrets.test.ts case asserts the throw for the default value in production.
- Non-production envs continue to work with the default.
close_reason: Added production guard rejecting built-in default cookieSecret; 3 new tests confirm throw in production, no-throw outside, no secret value in error message

title: Remediate: minPasswordLength=8 below NIST/OWASP 12-char minimum
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.ts:48

**Finding**:
better-auth emailAndPassword.minPasswordLength is set to 8, below the NIST 800-63B / OWASP 2025 recommendation of 12 characters. The single admin credential is the entire attack surface for the web app, so a shorter minimum meaningfully weakens brute-force resistance even with the DB-backed rate limit (10/min on /sign-in/email).

**Fix suggestion**:
Change minPasswordLength from 8 to 12 in src/server/auth/auth.ts. Confirm the seeded adminPassword in secrets.json meets the new minimum before re-running the seed migration.

**Acceptance**:
- src/server/auth/auth.ts sets minPasswordLength: 12
- A sign-in/sign-up attempt with an 11-char password is rejected
- Existing auth integration tests still pass
close_reason: Implementation committed by ralph-worker at 35bd845 (minPasswordLength=12 + startup/migration guards + tests); worker stopped before closing. Closed by orchestrator after confirming commit + clean tree.

title: Remediate: no trustedOrigins configured for better-auth (origin/CSRF pinning)
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.ts:35-80, src/server/serverApp.ts:33-34

**Finding**:
The betterAuth() config sets no trustedOrigins. With app.set('trust proxy', 1) and baseURL derived from BETTER_AUTH_URL, cross-origin request protection relies solely on the default baseURL match. A misconfigured or overly permissive BETTER_AUTH_URL could weaken better-auth's origin/CSRF checks for state-changing auth routes.

**Fix suggestion**:
Add an explicit trustedOrigins array to the betterAuth config pinned to the production public origin, e.g. trustedOrigins: process.env.BETTER_AUTH_URL ? [process.env.BETTER_AUTH_URL] : []. Verify sign-in still works from the legitimate origin and is rejected from a foreign Origin header.

**Acceptance**:
- betterAuth config in src/server/auth/auth.ts includes trustedOrigins pinned to BETTER_AUTH_URL in production
- A POST to /api/auth/sign-in/email with a foreign Origin header is rejected
- Existing auth integration tests still pass
close_reason: Added trustedOrigins pinned to BETTER_AUTH_URL; added integration test for foreign-origin 403

title: Remediate: weak default adminPassword can be seeded into production
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/util/secrets.ts:27-47, src/server/util/sampleSecrets.ts:1-6, migrations/1780760814405-SeedAdminUser.js

**Finding**:
defaultSecrets/sampleSecrets ship adminPassword 'yo' / 'password'. secrets.ts guards cookieSecret (length + not-default in production) but applies no equivalent guard to adminPassword. An operator who runs yarn migrate without overriding secrets.json seeds a production admin with a trivially guessable password. Related to closed 47t.10 (cookieSecret default guard) but adminPassword is currently unguarded.

**Fix suggestion**:
In src/server/util/secrets.ts, add a production fail-fast guard: throw if adminPassword equals the built-in default/sample value or is shorter than 12 characters (mirror the existing cookieSecret guard). Never log the value, only the field name. Keep the dev/test default convenient.

**Acceptance**:
- secrets.ts throws in production when adminPassword is the default/sample value or < 12 chars
- Error message names the field but never logs its value
- Dev/test startup is unaffected
- secrets.test.ts covers the new guard
close_reason: Added production fail-fast guard rejecting built-in default adminPassword; 3 new tests confirm throw in production, no-throw outside, value never in error message

title: Remediate: no HTTP security headers (HSTS/clickjacking/nosniff) on auth-serving Express app
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/serverApp.ts

**Finding**:
The Express app serves the authenticated admin SPA and now hosts the better-auth session-cookie surface, but sets no HTTP security response headers. Missing Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors (clickjacking protection for the signed-in admin UI), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, and a Content-Security-Policy. The gap predates this branch, but this branch is what introduces the authenticated production surface that these headers protect.

**Fix suggestion**:
Add helmet (or an equivalent header middleware) early in serverApp(), before the static and SPA-fallback handlers. At minimum enable: HSTS (production only), frameguard/frame-ancestors 'self' (clickjacking), noSniff, and a baseline CSP scoped to the SPA's asset/connect origins. Verify it does not break the React bundle or the /api/auth/* and /webified static routes.

**Acceptance**:
- Response headers on a production-mode GET / include Strict-Transport-Security, X-Frame-Options (or CSP frame-ancestors), and X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff.
- A baseline Content-Security-Policy is present and the SPA loads without CSP violations.
- /api/auth/* and /webified routes continue to function.
close_reason: Added helmet v8 middleware to serverApp: nosniff, SAMEORIGIN frameguard, baseline CSP, HSTS in production only; 4 TDD tests green

title: Remediate: stale /api/users/* entries in core API contract after usersController removal
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/core/interfaces/Api.ts, src/core/models/User.ts

**Finding**:
Three routes removed from the server (/api/users/current, /api/users/login, /api/users/logout) still appear in the core-layer APIGet/APIPost interfaces. The LoginAttempt type in User.ts is also dead — only referenced by the stale Api.ts entries. Dead entries in the core contract mislead future callers and, if any typed API client enumerates routes, could cause phantom calls to non-existent endpoints.

**Fix suggestion**:
1. Remove '/api/users/current' from APIGet.
2. Remove '/api/users/login' and '/api/users/logout' from APIPost.
3. Remove LoginAttempt import from Api.ts.
4. Remove the LoginAttempt interface from core/models/User.ts (unused after step 3).
5. Run yarn typecheck to confirm nothing depends on the removed entries.

**Acceptance**:
- Api.ts contains no /api/users/* entries.
- User.ts exports only the User interface.
- yarn typecheck passes with no errors.
close_reason: Removed /api/users/current from APIGet, /api/users/login and /api/users/logout from APIPost, and LoginAttempt from User.ts; core typecheck passes

title: Remediate: common/ auth thunks use synchronous require() to web/auth/authClient, pulling better-auth into desktop bundle
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.ts:44

**Finding**:
The shared common/ layer calls `require('../../web/auth/authClient')` (synchronous CommonJS require) inside a function closure. Webpack statically resolves synchronous require() calls during bundling, so better-auth/react and authClient will be included in the desktop bundle even though the desktop frontend never calls loadCurrentUser(), pushLogin(), or pushLogout(). This violates the dependency direction (common/ must not depend on web/) and defeats the isolation between web and desktop builds. The three auth thunk functions are only ever called from web/ components.

**Fix suggestion**:
Move loadCurrentUser(), pushLogin(), and pushLogout() out of currentUserSlice.ts (common/) and into a new file at src/frontend/web/auth/authThunks.ts (web/). The common/ currentUserSlice keeps only the reducer and state shape. Update imports in MainRouter.tsx, PublicHome.tsx, and AdminHome.tsx to import the thunks from web/auth/authThunks instead of common/state/currentUserSlice.

**Acceptance**:
- src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.ts contains no import, require, or reference to web/auth/authClient.
- The desktop webpack bundle (dist/frontend/desktop.bundle.js) does not include better-auth or authClient source.
- All existing tests for currentUserSlice pass.
- Web auth flows (sign-in, sign-out, session load) continue to function end-to-end.
close_reason: Moved auth thunks from common/state/currentUserSlice to web/auth/authThunks; currentUserSlice now contains only reducer and state shape with no web/ dependencies

title: Remediate: tighten CSP styleSrc to remove 'unsafe-inline'
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/serverApp.ts:54

**Finding**:
[MINOR] The Content-Security-Policy sets styleSrc to ['self', 'unsafe-inline']. Permitting inline styles weakens the XSS defense the CSP is meant to provide: an attacker who can inject markup can apply styles (used in CSS-based exfiltration / UI-redressing) without tripping the policy.

**Fix suggestion**:
Remove 'unsafe-inline' from styleSrc. If the React/web build emits inline <style> or style= attributes that require it, either (a) move those styles into bundled stylesheets served from 'self', or (b) adopt a nonce/hash-based styleSrc (helmet supports a per-request nonce via a CSP directive function). Verify the SPA renders correctly afterward.

**Acceptance**:
- styleSrc no longer contains 'unsafe-inline' (uses 'self' plus nonce/hash if needed)
- Web frontend renders without CSP style violations in the browser console
- serverApp.test.ts covers the tightened directive
close_reason: removed 'unsafe-inline' from styleSrc; added regression test; pushed 796a5c5

title: Remediate: seed migration duplicates Argon2id hash params from passwordHasher.ts (drift risk)
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: migrations/1780760814405-SeedAdminUser.js, src/server/auth/passwordHasher.ts

**Finding**:
The seed migration (migrations/1780760814405-SeedAdminUser.js:20-37) contains an inline `hashPassword()` function that duplicates the Argon2id parameters (MEMORY=19456, ITERATIONS=2, PARALLELISM=1, TAG_LENGTH=32, format string) from `src/server/auth/passwordHasher.ts`. If the runtime hash parameters are ever tuned (e.g. MEMORY bumped for OWASP compliance), the migration's standalone copy will silently produce credentials that cannot be re-verified, and existing seeded users will be unable to log in.

**Fix suggestion**:
The migration is CommonJS and runs at deploy time, while passwordHasher.ts is ESM. The simplest safe approach:
1. Extract the shared Argon2id constants (MEMORY, ITERATIONS, PARALLELISM, TAG_LENGTH, format string) into a small `migrations/_argon2Params.js` CommonJS module.
2. Have `migrations/1780760814405-SeedAdminUser.js` require() that constants file instead of hardcoding values.
3. Have `src/server/auth/passwordHasher.ts` either re-export those constants or add a jsdoc cross-reference comment that explicitly names the migrations file so any param change triggers a visible dual-site update.

**Acceptance**:
- A single source of truth for Argon2id parameters shared between migration and runtime hasher
- Changing MEMORY in one place is immediately visible at the other (either via import or prominent comment)
- Existing test coverage for passwordHasher.ts continues to pass
close_reason: Extracted Argon2id params to migrations/_argon2Params.js as single source of truth; migration and test now require() shared constants

title: Remediate: pushLogin silently discards non-401 errors (400, 429, 500, network)
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/web/auth/authThunks.ts

**Finding**:
src/frontend/web/auth/authThunks.ts:24 — pushLogin only handles result.error.status === 401 with setError. All other error paths (400 validation, 429 rate-limit, 500 server error, thrown network rejection) fall through silently: no dispatch is called, the user sees no feedback, and unhandled promise rejections may bubble uncaught.

**Fix suggestion**:
1. Add an else-if for any result.error (not just 401) that calls setError with a generic fallback message: 
2. Wrap the entire thunk body in try/catch and dispatch setError on rejection.
3. Add a test case: authClient.signIn.email rejects with a network error → setError is dispatched.

**Acceptance**:
- Non-401 errors (400, 429, 500) cause setError to be dispatched with a user-visible message
- Network-level rejection (Promise.reject) causes setError to be dispatched
- Unit tests cover each case
close_reason: Duplicate of lessons-from-luke-47t.20 (identical pushLogin non-401 error-handling finding, same file/fix). The code-quality review created the task twice; .20 is the one referenced in .sp-harden-findings.json. Closing .19 to avoid double-work in the harden drain.

title: Remediate: logout reducer does not clear error state (stale login-failed banner)
description: Review: sp:code-quality-review. Range: master..HEAD. Files: src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.ts. Finding: The logout reducer (line 32) sets user to null but does not clear the error field. If a user fails to log in, sees the login-failed alert, then the error is cleared via setUser but not via logout — any code path that dispatches logout directly (e.g. after session expiry) leaves the stale error in Redux state, potentially showing a misleading login-failed banner on next render. Fix: Add state.error = null to the logout reducer. Add a test: dispatch setError then logout, assert error is null after logout. Acceptance: logout reducer clears error field; test added.
close_reason: logout reducer now clears error field; test added confirming stale banner cannot reappear

title: Remediate: passwordHasher.verify uses hardcoded TAG_LENGTH not encoded in hash format string
description: Review: sp:code-quality-review. Range: master..HEAD. Files: src/server/auth/passwordHasher.ts. Finding: verify() uses the module-level TAG_LENGTH constant (32) when re-hashing rather than deriving tag length from the stored hash. The custom format string (argon2id dollar m dollar t dollar p dollar saltHex dollar hashHex) does not encode tag length, so a hash produced with TAG_LENGTH=32 cannot be re-verified if TAG_LENGTH is later changed to e.g. 64 — existing credentials silently break. Note: the length check (actualHashBuf.length \!== expectedHashBuf.length) catches mismatches, but it returns false rather than signalling the mismatch clearly. Fix: Either encode tag length in the hash format string (add a 7th field) so verify can round-trip it, OR document a migration policy. Also related to task lessons-from-luke-47t.18 (param drift). Acceptance: TAG_LENGTH change does not silently break existing credential verification OR format string encodes tag length.
close_reason: hash() now encodes tag length as 7th field; verify() derives tagLength from stored hash, backward-compat with legacy 6-field hashes retained

title: Remediate: entrypoint.sh seeds adminPassword "yo" which fails the new 12-char startup guard
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: entrypoint.sh (line ~29)

**Finding**:
The Docker entrypoint auto-generates secrets.json with adminPassword: "yo" (3 chars) and cookieSecret: "dev-only-secret-replace-in-production-xx". The new validation in src/server/util/secrets.ts:75 throws when adminPassword.length < 12, and migrations/1780760814405-SeedAdminUser.js:53 throws the same. A fresh Docker dev container will therefore crash at server startup and at migration time. Dev-only (NODE_ENV \!= production fails closed); no production exposure, but blocks the documented Docker workflow.

**Fix suggestion**:
Update the heredoc in entrypoint.sh to seed an adminPassword of >= 12 chars (e.g. "dev-password-1" matching defaultSecrets in secrets.ts). Optionally drop the now-unused adminUsername field to avoid confusion since auth keys on adminEmail.

**Acceptance**:
- A fresh `docker compose up -d --build` produces a secrets.json whose adminPassword is >= 12 chars
- `yarn migrate:test` / server startup no longer throw the adminPassword length error
- adminEmail present and consistent with seed migration
close_reason: entrypoint.sh now seeds adminPassword dev-password-1 (14 chars); adminUsername removed; fresh docker compose up no longer crashes on startup guard

title: Remediate: Api.ts declares /api/auth/get-session with incomplete return type
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/core/interfaces/Api.ts:25

**Finding**:
The core-layer type contract declares `"/api/auth/get-session": [Record<string, never>, { user: User } | null]` but better-auth's actual response shape is `{ session: Session, user: User }` (not `{ user: User } | null`). The route is used only as a connectivity probe in networkSlice.ts (return value discarded), so there is no runtime bug — but the declared type is misleading and the simplified `{ user: User } | null` shape does not match what the server actually returns.

**Fix suggestion**:
Update the declared return type to match better-auth's actual GET /api/auth/get-session shape: `{ session: { id: string; userId: string; expiresAt: string }; user: User } | null`. Alternatively, since this route is only probed for connectivity, replace the route entry with a dedicated `/api/auth/probe` that intentionally returns `null | {}` to make its purpose explicit.

**Acceptance**:
- The declared response type for /api/auth/get-session in Api.ts matches the actual better-auth response structure
- Or a dedicated probe endpoint replaces the session route for reconnection detection
- TypeScript strict-mode compilation passes with no casts needed at the call site
close_reason: Api.ts /api/auth/get-session return type updated to match better-auth actual response shape with session field

title: Remediate: PublicHome test does not assert the login-failed alert actually renders
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/web/home/PublicHome.test.tsx:60-80

**Finding**:
The test titled 'shows login failed alert when credentials are invalid (401 error)' mocks authClient.signIn.email to return an error, clicks the login button, then only asserts `expect(container).toBeTruthy()`. It does not verify that the 'Login failed' alert actually appears in the DOM. The comment 'Component renders without crashing; error state is in Redux' confirms the test was intentionally weakened. Since PublicHome reads `currentUser.error` from the store and renders `{loginFailed && <Alert danger>{t('Log_in_failed')}</Alert>}`, a regression that breaks this render path would not be caught.

**Fix suggestion**:
Replace the `expect(container).toBeTruthy()` assertion with a query for the failure alert text. Example:
```ts
const { getByText } = renderWithProviders(<PublicHome />, {
  syncState: defaultSyncState,
  currentUser: { user: null, locale: 'en', loaded: false, error: null },
});
await act(async () => { fireEvent.click(loginButton\!); });
expect(getByText(/log.?in failed/i)).toBeInTheDocument();
```
Note: the preloadedState for currentUser should include `error: null` so RTK doesn't leave the field undefined.

**Acceptance**:
- The test asserts that the 'Login failed' / 'Log_in_failed' alert is present in the DOM after a failed login
- A deliberate regression (e.g. removing the `{loginFailed && <Alert>` line) causes the test to fail
close_reason: test now asserts login-failed alert renders in DOM via getByText; weak expect(container).toBeTruthy replaced

title: SETUP: Harden secrets.ts and sampleSecrets.ts (fail-fast config)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-011, FR-003; plan.md §Technical Context, research.md §Decision 7

title: US1 [GREEN]: Implement passwordHasher.ts (Argon2id)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-001, SC-003; plan.md §Project Structure; research.md §Decision 2
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/passwordHasher.ts (NEW)

**Context**: Make the RED tests in lessons-from-luke-47t.5.5.1 pass. Use @noble/hashes@^2.2.0 with v2 import paths.

**CRITICAL import paths** (v2, with .js suffix — NOT v1 paths):
  import { argon2id } from '@noble/hashes/argon2.js'
  import { randomBytes, bytesToHex } from '@noble/hashes/utils.js'

If you use '@noble/hashes/argon2' (without .js), Node will throw ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED at runtime.

**Implementation**:
export function hashPassword(password: string): string
  - salt = randomBytes(16)
  - hashBytes = argon2id(password, salt, { t: 2, m: 19456, p: 1, dkLen: 32 })
  - return 'argon2id$19456$2$1$' + bytesToHex(salt) + '$' + bytesToHex(hashBytes)

export function verifyPassword(storedHash: string, password: string): boolean
  - Parse the argon2id$m$t$p$saltHex$hashHex format
  - If parsing fails, return false (do not throw)
  - Recompute hash with same salt + parameters
  - Return constant-time comparison (compare hex strings is acceptable since hex encoding is already fixed-length)

JSDoc comments on both exported functions.
Explicit TypeScript return types: string, boolean.

**Acceptance**: npx jest --testPathPattern=passwordHasher --no-coverage shows PASS (all 5 tests green).
close_reason: passwordHasher.ts implemented using Node 24 crypto.argon2Sync; all 5 tests green

title: US1 [GREEN]: Create DDL migration (AddBetterAuthTables)
description:

**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: migrations/<unix-ms>-AddBetterAuthTables.js (NEW)

**Context**: Creates the five better-auth tables. Uses existing makeDbConnect porsager helper. Column names are dictated by better-auth and MUST NOT be renamed. Uses IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency. Wraps all DDL in a transaction.

**Create order** (FK targets first): user → session → account → verification → rateLimit → indexes
**Drop order** (reverse): rateLimit → verification → account → session → user

**Tables to create** (exact columns per data-model.md):
- user: id text PK, name text NOT NULL, email text NOT NULL UNIQUE, emailVerified boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false, image text, admin boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false, createdAt timestamptz NOT NULL, updatedAt timestamptz NOT NULL
- session: id text PK, userId text NOT NULL REFERENCES user(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, token text NOT NULL UNIQUE, expiresAt timestamptz NOT NULL, ipAddress text, userAgent text, createdAt/updatedAt timestamptz NOT NULL
- account: id text PK, userId text NOT NULL REFERENCES user(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, accountId text NOT NULL, providerId text NOT NULL, accessToken/refreshToken/idToken text nullable, accessTokenExpiresAt/refreshTokenExpiresAt timestamptz nullable, scope text nullable, password text nullable, createdAt/updatedAt timestamptz NOT NULL
- verification: id text PK, identifier text NOT NULL, value text NOT NULL, expiresAt timestamptz NOT NULL, createdAt/updatedAt timestamptz nullable
- rateLimit: id text PK, key text NOT NULL, count integer NOT NULL, lastRequest bigint NOT NULL

**Indexes**: idx_account_userId on account(userId), idx_session_userId on session(userId), idx_verification_identifier on verification(identifier)

IMPORTANT: Verify exact column names against installed better-auth@1.6.14 by checking node_modules/better-auth before writing DDL — the installed schema is authoritative.

**Acceptance**:

- psql: all 5 tables exist with correct columns
- Re-running yarn migrate:test is idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS guards)

title: US1 [GREEN]: Create idempotent seed migration (SeedAdminUser)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-002, FR-003, US4; data-model.md §Migration DDL ordering; plan.md §Security Considerations (Bootstrap); research.md §Decision 10
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: migrations/<unix-ms+1>-SeedAdminUser.js (NEW — timestamp must be 1ms after AddBetterAuthTables)

**Context**: Inserts one admin user from secrets.json. Idempotent: skips if admin email already exists. Throws loudly if adminEmail is missing. Atomically inserts both user AND account rows to prevent un-loginable admin state. Uses same Argon2id format as passwordHasher.ts.

**CRITICAL security requirements** (red-team Pass 1+2):
1. NEVER log the plaintext adminPassword or echo secrets.json contents on error
2. Log only which field is missing, never its value
3. Insert user + account in a SINGLE TRANSACTION (atomicity) — partial seed (user without account) creates an un-loginable admin that a re-run silently skips
4. Idempotency check MUST verify BOTH a user AND a matching credential account exist before skipping
5. Store adminEmail in LOWERCASE (normalized) — better-auth treats email case-insensitively at login but the UNIQUE constraint is on the stored value

**Argon2id import** (CommonJS, v2 paths with .js suffix):
  const argon2id = require('@noble/hashes/argon2.js').argon2id
  const randomBytes = require('@noble/hashes/utils.js').randomBytes
  const bytesToHex = require('@noble/hashes/utils.js').bytesToHex

**Logic**:
1. Read secrets.json; throw if adminEmail missing (with field name only in error, not value)
2. Check if user with adminEmail (lowercased) AND account with providerId='credential' linked to that user already exist
3. If both exist: log 'admin account already exists, skipping seed' and return
4. If neither exists: in a single transaction, INSERT user (id=crypto.randomUUID(), email=adminEmail.toLowerCase(), name='Admin', admin=true, emailVerified=false, createdAt=now, updatedAt=now) then INSERT account (id=crypto.randomUUID(), userId=user.id, accountId=user.id, providerId='credential', password=hashPassword(adminPassword), createdAt=now, updatedAt=now)
5. hashPassword must produce same format as passwordHasher.ts: 'argon2id$19456$2$1$' + bytesToHex(salt) + '$' + bytesToHex(hashBytes)
6. Throw clearly if adminPassword is missing (field name only, not value)

**Acceptance**:
- yarn migrate:test succeeds; psql: SELECT email, admin FROM "user" → one row, admin=true
- SELECT providerId, left(password, 8) FROM account → providerId='credential', starts 'argon2id'
- Re-running yarn migrate:test: logs 'skipping' message, no duplicate, no error
- Temporarily removing adminEmail from secrets.json: migration fails with clear message (no secrets value logged)
- email stored in lowercase
close_reason: SeedAdminUser migration created: atomically inserts admin user+account, idempotent check on both rows, lowercase email, no secret values logged on error

title: US1 [GREEN]: Create auth.ts factory (getAuth/resetAuth)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-001, FR-002, FR-006, FR-010, FR-012; plan.md §Project Structure; research.md §Decision 1, 3, 7, 8
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.ts (NEW)

**Context**: getAuth() creates the better-auth instance backed by an isolated pg.Pool (separate from the domain porsager driver). This is declarative glue — excluded from unit coverage per jest.config.js (lessons-from-luke-47t.5.3); its behavior is covered by auth.integration.test.ts.

**What to create** src/server/auth/auth.ts:

Import: import { betterAuth } from 'better-auth'
Import: import { Pool } from 'pg'
Import: secrets from src/server/util/secrets.ts

Pool configuration:
- new Pool({ ...secrets.db, max: 5 }) in production
- new Pool({ ...secrets.testDb, max: 5 }) in test (NODE_ENV=test)
- new Pool({ ...secrets.devDb, max: 5 }) in dev
- max: 5 — the porsager driver defaults to os.cpus() (≤4 on this VPS); combined ceiling ≤9, well under postgres max_connections=100

better-auth configuration:
- database: the pg.Pool (better-auth's built-in Kysely adapter handles it — no drizzle, no explicit kysely)
- secret: secrets.cookieSecret (validated ≥32 chars in secrets.ts)
- baseURL: process.env.BETTER_AUTH_URL ?? 'http://localhost:8081'
- emailAndPassword: { enabled: true, disableSignUp: true, minPasswordLength: 8, maxPasswordLength: 128 }
  IMPORTANT: maxPasswordLength MUST be explicitly set (not relying on unverified library default — red-team Pass 2)
- rateLimit: { enabled: true, storage: 'database', customRules: { '/sign-in/email': { window: 60, max: 10 } } }
  DB-backed rate limiter so limits are not per-worker under Passenger (red-team Pass 1)
- additionalFields on user: { admin: { type: 'boolean', defaultValue: false, input: false } }
  input: false means admin cannot be set via any public API (only the seed/raw SQL sets admin: true)

Export: getAuth() — returns the singleton auth instance (call once, cache it)
Export: resetAuth() — for test isolation: nulls out the singleton so test setup can re-create with a fresh pool

**Acceptance**:
- TypeScript strict: no errors in src/server/tsconfig.json for this file
- File is excluded from unit coverage per jest.config.js
- getAuth() returns a better-auth instance; calling it twice returns the same instance
- resetAuth() causes the next getAuth() call to create a new instance
close_reason: auth.ts factory created: getAuth() singleton with DB-backed rate-limit, maxPasswordLength=128, resetAuth() for test isolation; typecheck clean

title: US1 [GREEN]: Create auth.integration.test.ts (sign-in/session/sign-out flows)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US1, US2, US3; plan.md §Testing; research.md §Decision 5, 6; quickstart.md §Integration tests
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.integration.test.ts (NEW), src/server/testHelper.ts (MODIFY), src/server/jestSetupAfterEnv.ts (MODIFY)

**Context**: Integration test file covering the full auth lifecycle against the real test DB (lessons-from-luke-test). These tests run with the integration job (yarn test:integration), NOT the unit job. They exercise getAuth() + the actual pg.Pool.

**jestSetupAfterEnv.ts modifications** (test isolation — research Decision 5, red-team Pass 1+3):
After each test's domain rollback, add cleanup of auth tables on the auth pool:
  DELETE FROM "rateLimit"    -- clears rate-limit counts (prevents order-dependent throttle leakage)
  DELETE FROM "session"      -- clears sessions created in tests
  DELETE FROM "verification" -- clears verification tokens
  DELETE FROM account WHERE "userId" IN (SELECT id FROM "user" WHERE email \!= '<adminEmail-lowercase>')
  DELETE FROM "user" WHERE email \!= '<adminEmail-lowercase>'
The cleanup MUST spare the seeded admin (compare on lowercase email). MUST run even when the test body throws (afterEach, not inline teardown).

**testHelper.ts modification**:
loggedInAgent() currently POSTs to /api/users/login with username/password.
Change to POST to /api/auth/sign-in/email with { email: secrets.adminEmail, password: secrets.adminPassword }.

**auth.integration.test.ts** — test cases (against real test DB, real better-auth):
1. GET /api/auth/get-session unauthenticated → null (no session)
2. POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with wrong password → 401, no session created (US1 scenario 2; no enumeration)
3. POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with correct admin creds → 200, session cookie set; GET /api/auth/get-session → admin:true user (US1 scenario 1)
4. POST /api/auth/sign-out after login → 200, cookie cleared; GET /api/auth/get-session → null (US3)
5. GET /api/admin/languages without session → 401 (US2 scenario 1)
6. GET /api/admin/languages with non-admin session (insert non-admin via raw SQL on auth pool) → 403 (US2 scenario 2)
7. GET /api/admin/languages with admin loggedInAgent() → 200 (US2 scenario 3)
8. Repeated bad sign-in (>10 attempts) → 429 Too Many Requests (rate-limit assertion — red-team Pass 1); reset rateLimit rows after
9. POST /api/auth/sign-up/email → rejected/403 (FR-002, US4 scenario 4)
10. POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with overlong password (e.g. 200 chars) → 400 (max-length guard — red-team Pass 2)

**Acceptance**:
- yarn test:integration shows all 10 tests pass
- Tests run twice in any order: no session-row leakage (isolation verified)
- No test leaves orphaned rows in user/session/account/verification/rateLimit
close_reason: Work was complete and pushed (commits be87cde..8485f51); auth.integration.test.ts passes 11/11. The earlier RECOVERED was a false negative from a stale .migrate-test state file (AddBetterAuthTables marked run before the rateLimit table was added to it), now fixed by re-running the two idempotent auth migrations against the test DB.

title: Remediate: fail-fast on missing BETTER_AUTH_URL in production (insecure cookies)
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.ts:37, src/server/util/secrets.ts:53-68

**Finding** (HIGH):
In production, when BETTER_AUTH_URL is unset, baseURL falls back to "http://localhost:8081". better-auth keys the Secure / __Host- session-cookie attributes off an https baseURL, so a missing/non-https BETTER_AUTH_URL silently downgrades the session cookie to be sent over plaintext and drops the __Host- host-lock. The startup fail-fast in secrets.ts validates cookieSecret and adminEmail but NOT BETTER_AUTH_URL, so a misconfigured deploy boots with insecure cookies and no warning.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Add a fail-fast guard (alongside the existing checks in secrets.ts or in auth.ts getAuth()): when NODE_ENV === "production", require process.env.BETTER_AUTH_URL to be set and to start with "https://"; throw a clear startup error otherwise (never log the value).
- Optionally set advanced.useSecureCookies: true explicitly in production so cookie security does not depend solely on baseURL scheme detection.

**Acceptance**:
- Starting the server in production with BETTER_AUTH_URL unset or non-https throws a clear configuration error at startup.
- With BETTER_AUTH_URL=https://... in production, the session cookie is __Host-better-auth.session_token with Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax; Path=/ (matches quickstart.md section 7).
- New unit test in secrets.test.ts (or auth test) covers the production guard.
close_reason: Added production fail-fast guard for BETTER_AUTH_URL (must be https://); set useSecureCookies: true in auth.ts; 5 new tests in secrets.test.ts

title: Remediate: Argon2id passwordHasher is dead code; runtime uses scrypt (docs/impl mismatch)
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/passwordHasher.ts, src/server/auth/auth.ts (emailAndPassword config), migrations/1780760814405-SeedAdminUser.js

**Finding** (MEDIUM):
src/server/auth/passwordHasher.ts implements Argon2id hash/verify but is imported nowhere except its own test. auth.ts never wires it into better-auth (no emailAndPassword.password = { hash, verify } override), so the actual runtime password hashing is better-auth built-in scrypt (which is why SeedAdminUser.js hashes with scrypt N=16384). The spec, research.md, and CLAUDE.md all claim passwords use "Argon2id", but the live verifier is scrypt and the Argon2id module is unused dead security code. scrypt N=16384 is acceptable (not a weak hash), so this is a documentation/implementation mismatch and a maintenance hazard, not an exploitable crypto weakness.

**Fix suggestion** (pick one):
- (Preferred) Wire passwordHasher into better-auth: set emailAndPassword.password = { hash, verify } in auth.ts, and update SeedAdminUser.js to produce the matching Argon2id "argon2id$m$t$p$saltHex$hashHex" format so the seeded admin can still log in. Re-run the seed/migration verification.
- OR delete passwordHasher.ts + its test and correct spec.md/research.md/CLAUDE.md to state scrypt, so docs match reality and no misleading unused crypto remains.

**Acceptance**:
- No unused custom password hasher remains, OR it is actually invoked by better-auth at runtime.
- The seeded admin can sign in successfully under whichever algorithm is chosen (integration test green).
- Documentation (spec/research/CLAUDE.md) accurately names the password hash algorithm in use.
close_reason: Wired Argon2id passwordHasher into better-auth emailAndPassword config; updated SeedAdminUser migration to use argon2Sync with legacy scrypt re-seed; docs updated

title: Remediate: pushLogin hardcodes admin:true instead of reading server role
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.ts:74-77

**Finding** (LOW):
After a successful sign-in, pushLogin dispatches setUser({ id, admin: true }) — it hardcodes admin: true for every authenticated user rather than reading result.data.user.admin. loadCurrentUser correctly derives admin via Boolean(admin), so the two paths disagree. Server-side requireAdmin still enforces real authorization (the server is the source of truth), so this is client-state only and not a privilege-escalation vuln; impact is currently low because signup is disabled and only the seeded admin exists. But the inconsistency will mislead any future non-admin user (e.g. the planned invitation provisioning, US04) into a UI that assumes admin.

**Fix suggestion**:
Mirror loadCurrentUser: read the role from the response, e.g.
  const { id, admin } = result.data.user as { id: string; admin?: boolean };
  dispatch(currentUserSlice.actions.setUser({ id, admin: Boolean(admin) }));

**Acceptance**:
- pushLogin sets admin from the server-returned user.admin, not a hardcoded true.
- currentUserSlice.test.ts asserts a non-admin sign-in yields admin: false.
close_reason: pushLogin now reads admin from server response via Boolean(admin); non-admin tests added

title: Remediate: serverApp.ts creates a second pg.Pool for invitations instead of sharing auth.ts's pool
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/serverApp.ts, src/server/auth/auth.ts

**Finding**:
`serverApp.ts` creates its own `authPool = new Pool(..., max:3)` for invitation routes. `auth.ts`'s `getAuth()` independently creates another `Pool` (`max:5`) inside the singleton. The result is two separate pg.Pool instances connected to the same auth database, totalling up to 8 connections for auth-related operations. CLAUDE.md and plan.md both state invitations use "the same isolated pg.Pool" that better-auth uses, but the implementation silently diverges from that documented design. The connection budget is safe (8 << 100) but the architecture comment is misleading and the pool lifecycle is not coordinated (e.g. no shared drain on shutdown).

**Fix suggestion**:
- Export the pool from `auth.ts` (e.g. a `getAuthPool(): Pool` function alongside `getAuth()`) so `serverApp.ts` and the invitation controller can share a single pool instance.
- Remove the duplicate `authPool` creation from `serverApp.ts` and update the `invitationController` call sites to use the shared pool.
- Update CLAUDE.md's "Active Technologies" note to reflect the actual connection architecture.

**Acceptance**:
- Only one `pg.Pool` instance is created for auth-database operations at runtime.
- All invitation controller tests and integration tests still pass.
- `auth.ts` exports `getAuthPool()` or equivalent; `serverApp.ts` no longer creates its own `Pool`.
close_reason: Extracted getAuthPool() singleton from auth.ts; serverApp.ts now uses it instead of creating a second Pool

title: Remediate: anonymous invitation rate-limit key trusts spoofable X-Forwarded-For
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts (invitationRateLimit, line ~164), src/server/serverApp.ts (line 37)

**Finding**:
The per-IP rate limiter keys on req.ip with app.set('trust proxy', 1). Express then derives req.ip from the LEFT-most X-Forwarded-For entry, trusting exactly one proxy hop. If the production reverse proxy (Passenger/nginx) does not overwrite a client-supplied X-Forwarded-For header, an attacker can rotate the spoofed IP per request and fully evade the 10-req/60s limit on the anonymous /api/auth/invitation/:token lookup and /accept endpoints, enabling unthrottled token brute-force and account-creation attempts.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Confirm and document that the deploy proxy strips/overwrites inbound X-Forwarded-For before forwarding; if it appends instead, adjust 'trust proxy' to the exact trusted hop count or a subnet, not the integer 1.
- Add a deployment note (quickstart.md / config) asserting the proxy contract the rate limiter depends on.
- Optionally fall back to a coarser key (e.g. token-hash-scoped attempts) so the limiter degrades safely if req.ip is unreliable.

**Acceptance**:
- A request carrying a forged X-Forwarded-For cannot reset its own rate-limit bucket in the deployed configuration (verified against the real proxy or documented as enforced by it).
- Deployment doc states the X-Forwarded-For trust contract.
close_reason: Added deployment contract comment in serverApp.ts (proxy strips X-Forwarded-For) and token-scoped secondary rate-limit key for GET /api/auth/invitation/:token as graceful degradation

title: Remediate: admin create-invitation route is an account-enumeration oracle with no rate limit
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts (POST /api/admin/invitations, lines 333-388; createInvitation in src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts lines 199-205)

**Finding**:
POST /api/admin/invitations returns a distinct 409 with code ACCOUNT_EXISTS when a user account already exists for the submitted email, versus 201 otherwise. The route carries requireAdmin and requireSameOrigin but NO rate limiter (the invitationRateLimit middleware is applied only to the anonymous routes). A signed-in admin — or a hijacked/compromised admin session, or a curious lower-trust admin — can probe arbitrary emails to learn which addresses have registered accounts (user-enumeration oracle). Low severity because it requires an authenticated admin, but it leaks PII presence across the user base.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Decide whether account-existence disclosure to admins is acceptable per spec; if not, collapse the AccountExistsError and ActivePendingError responses to a single non-distinguishing 409 message, OR
- Apply the existing invitationRateLimit (or a separate admin-scoped limiter) to the admin create route to bound enumeration speed, AND
- Ensure no enumeration signal is exposed beyond the admin trust boundary.

**Acceptance**:
- Either the create response no longer distinguishes 'account exists' from other 409 conflict reasons, or the route is rate-limited so bulk enumeration is impractical, with the chosen behavior documented in spec.md.
- A test asserts the chosen non-leaky / throttled behavior.
close_reason: rate-limit POST /api/admin/invitations with invitationRateLimit middleware; test 10 asserts 429; plan.md updated

title: Remediate: duplicate origin allow-list and BETTER_AUTH_URL default spread across auth.ts and invitationController.ts

title: Remediate: two async GET routes in invitationController.ts unguarded in Express 4
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts

**Finding**:
GET /api/auth/invitation/:token (line 248) and GET /api/admin/invitations (line 392) are async route handlers with no try/catch. Express 4 does NOT automatically propagate rejected async handler promises to the error handler. A DB failure (pool exhaustion, query error) will produce an unhandled promise rejection and leave the HTTP request hanging indefinitely, eventually timing out rather than returning a 500.

All other routes (POST /api/admin/invitations, POST retract, GET link) do wrap their async operations in try/catch.

**Fix suggestion**:
Wrap the body of each unguarded route in try/catch:
- GET /api/auth/invitation/:token: wrap lookupInvitation call in try/catch, rethrow unexpected errors (or use the project's existing handleErrors pattern from src/server/api/WebAPI.ts).
- GET /api/admin/invitations: wrap listInvitations call in try/catch, rethrow unexpected errors.

Alternatively, use the project's existing handleErrors() wrapper from WebAPI.ts to standardise all async route error handling across invitationController.

**Acceptance**:
- A simulated DB error on GET /api/auth/invitation/:token returns HTTP 500, not a hang.
- A simulated DB error on GET /api/admin/invitations returns HTTP 500, not a hang.
- No unhandled promise rejections emitted.
close_reason: Wrapped GET /api/auth/invitation/:token and GET /api/admin/invitations in try/catch; unexpected errors now call next(err) for proper Express 4 error propagation

title: Remediate: AccountExistsError and AccountAlreadyExistsError are confusingly named near-identical classes
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts

**Finding**:
Two error classes in invitationStore.ts serve the same conceptual purpose ("account already exists for this email") but have similar yet distinct names and different code strings:
- AccountExistsError (code: ACCOUNT_EXISTS) — thrown at create-invitation time when a user row already exists
- AccountAlreadyExistsError (code: ACCOUNT_ALREADY_EXISTS) — thrown at accept-invitation time when a concurrent accept or duplicate-email race wins

The distinction matters (different flows, different HTTP status contexts), but the naming pattern ('Error' vs 'AlreadyExists' + 'Error') makes it easy to grab the wrong class. The invitationController.ts import list shows both, which adds cognitive load for future maintainers.

**Fix suggestion**:
Rename to make the flow-context explicit:
- AccountExistsError -> AccountAlreadyRegisteredError (with code: ACCOUNT_EXISTS preserved for API compatibility) — signifies 'a user account is registered for this email; cannot invite'
- AccountAlreadyExistsError -> AccountCreatedConcurrentlyError (with code: ACCOUNT_ALREADY_EXISTS preserved) — signifies 'a race at accept time; someone else won'

Or, if the distinction is intentional and clear enough, add a JSDoc comment to each explaining why two classes are needed and how they differ.

**Acceptance**:
- The two error classes have names that immediately communicate their usage context.
- No API-visible code strings change (ACCOUNT_EXISTS, ACCOUNT_ALREADY_EXISTS preserved).
- All usages in invitationStore.ts and invitationController.ts updated consistently.
close_reason: Renamed AccountExistsError→AccountAlreadyRegisteredError and AccountAlreadyExistsError→AccountCreatedConcurrentlyError; API codes unchanged; JSDoc added

title: Remediate: extract invitation middleware to middle/
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts:73,171

**Finding**:
Two middleware functions (requireSameOrigin, invitationRateLimit) are defined and exported from a controller file instead of the project's dedicated middleware directory (src/server/middle/). The existing requireUser.ts establishes the pattern that Express middleware lives in middle/. title: Remediate: dead _cookieSecret parameter in acceptInvitation signature
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts:401, src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts:346

**Finding**:
The `acceptInvitation` function declares a fifth parameter `_cookieSecret: string` that is never used inside the function body. The underscore prefix suppresses the ESLint no-unused-vars warning, but the parameter is still part of the public API signature. Every caller (including `invitationController.ts:346`) passes `secrets.cookieSecret`, which is wasted work and creates false impressions that the secret is needed at accept time.

Remove `_cookieSecret: string` from the `acceptInvitation` signature and update all call sites to stop passing the argument. The function accepts the token as a hash (looked up by `tokenHash`) and no longer needs `cookieSecret` for decryption at accept time.

title: Remediate: InvitationsList copySuccess state not reset before re-copy, suppressing aria-live re-announcement
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/web/invitations/InvitationsList.tsx:56-66

**Finding**:
`handleRecopy` sets `setCopySuccess(true)` but never resets it to `false` before the next copy attempt. Once `copySuccess` is `true`, subsequent clicks call `setState(true)` — React sees no state change, skips re-rendering the aria-live region, and the `aria-live="polite"` region never re-announces "Copied\!" for subsequent copies. The same pattern exists in `CreateInvitation.tsx` (line 46 resets it in `handleSubmit`, but not in `handleCopyLink`).

**Fix suggestion**:
In `handleRecopy`, reset `setCopySuccess(false)` before the async dispatch (or use a short `setTimeout` trick to force a DOM change). Simplest approach: `setCopySuccess(false)` at the top of `handleRecopy`, then `setCopySuccess(true)` on success — the double state update will force a re-render and re-announce.

**Acceptance**:
- Clicking "Re-copy Link" a second time on the same row still triggers the aria-live announcement
- Test updated or added to assert that re-clicking fires a second copy-success announcement
close_reason: reset copySuccess to false before re-copy so aria-live re-announces on every click; same fix applied to CreateInvitation.handleCopyLink; regression test added

title: US1 [GREEN]: Implement invitationTokens helpers
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-001, §FR-009, §FR-016

**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationTokens.ts (CREATE NEW)

## Task
Implement the invitationTokens module to make the RED tests pass (GREEN state). Do NOT over-engineer beyond what the tests require.

## Functions to implement
- `generateToken(): string` — crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('base64url'); 256-bit URL-safe single-use secret
- `hashToken(token: string): string` — crypto.createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex'); deterministic hex
- `encryptToken(token: string, secret: string): string` — AES-256-GCM with fresh 12-byte random IV; key = 32 bytes derived from secret via crypto.scryptSync(secret, fixedFeatureSalt, 32); persist as base64(iv):base64(authTag):base64(ciphertext)
- `decryptToken(encrypted: string, secret: string): string | null` — parse iv:authTag:ciphertext, verify GCM auth tag, return plaintext or null on failure (never throw a 500)
- `buildInvitationLink(token: string, baseUrl: string): string` — `${baseUrl}/invitation/${token}`

## Key requirements
- AES-256-GCM with fresh random 12-byte IV per row (NEVER a static/zero IV — GCM IV reuse is catastrophic)
- Key derived from cookieSecret via KDF (scryptSync with a fixed feature-scoped salt), not raw bytes
- Decrypt/auth failure returns null, not an exception (maps to 409 'link unavailable' at re-copy time)
- All functions must be exported with explicit TypeScript return types (strict mode)
- No `any` types

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationTokens.test.ts --runInBand` passes (GREEN state)
close_reason: Implemented invitationTokens module with generateToken, hashToken, encryptToken (AES-256-GCM), decryptToken (null on failure), buildInvitationLink

   - any other constraint → rethrow as generic error (→ 500)
8. Return: { id, email, role, status:'pending', link: buildInvitationLink(token, baseUrl), expiresAt }

description:

**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure (invitationStore.ts), data-model.md §Accept transaction & lazy-expire concurrency, §Validation rules, research.md §Decision 1 (direct SQL insert), §Decision 4
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing

## Task
Extend invitationStore.test.ts with failing tests for the lookup and accept paths. These tests will fail until invitationStore.ts implements the new functions.

## Test cases for lookupInvitation(pool, token)
1. Returns { email } for a valid pending, non-expired token (200 path)
2. Returns null for an unknown token (410 path)
3. Returns null for an accepted invitation (410 path — single-use, FR-009)
4. Returns null for a retracted invitation (410 path)
5. Returns null for an invitation past expiresAt even if status is still 'pending' (expired at read time, FR-018)

## Test cases for acceptInvitation(pool, token, password, name, cookieSecret)
1. Happy path: creates user+account row (via direct SQL on pg.Pool), flips invitation to 'accepted' with acceptedAt set, returns { email }
2. The created account can sign in with better-auth (FR-012 end-to-end latent coupling note — see plan.md Pass 8)
3. Invalid token (unknown) → throws InvalidLinkError (→ 410)
4. Already-accepted link → throws InvalidLinkError (→ 410, SC-003 single-use)
5. Retracted while form was open → throws InvalidLinkError (→ 410, spec Edge Cases)
6. Expired while form was open → throws InvalidLinkError (→ 410)
7. Password < 12 chars → throws ValidationError (validated before hashing, plan.md §Performance)
8. Password > 128 chars → throws ValidationError
9. Name empty after trim → throws ValidationError (red-team Pass 6)
10. Name with control characters/newlines → throws ValidationError (Pass 6)
11. Concurrent double-redemption: both calls race; exactly one creates an account, the other gets InvalidLinkError — single-use invariant (SC-003, spec Edge Cases)
12. Redeeming while carrying admin session context: account uses invitation's bound email and role, not the signed-in user's identity (spec Edge Case: already-signed-in visitor)

## Key implementation note for the accept path
- Accept MUST be an atomic conditional UPDATE: UPDATE invitation SET status='accepted', acceptedAt=now() WHERE id= AND status='pending' AND expiresAt > now() — a 0-row result is the 410 path
- User.admin set via direct SQL (the only way — auth.ts sets input:false for admin field)
- User.email stored lowercased; user.name trimmed, control chars rejected
- No session created at accept (FR-012)

title: US2 [GREEN]: Implement invitationStore lookup + accept paths
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-007..FR-012
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure, data-model.md §Accept transaction & lazy-expire concurrency, research.md §Decision 1, §Decision 4
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts (EDIT — add lookupInvitation and acceptInvitation)

## Task
Add lookupInvitation() and acceptInvitation() to invitationStore.ts to make the RED tests pass.

## lookupInvitation(pool: Pool, token: string): Promise<{ email: string } | null>
- Hash the token: hashToken(token) → tokenHash
- SELECT id, email, status, expiresAt FROM invitation WHERE tokenHash=$1
- If not found OR status \!= 'pending' OR expiresAt <= now(): return null (non-leaky, FR-010)
- If valid: return { email }
- Set Cache-Control: no-store on the response (controller sets the header, but the store returns data)

## acceptInvitation(pool: Pool, token: string, password: string, name: string, cookieSecret: string): Promise<{ email: string }>
1. Validate password: length 12..128 BEFORE hashing (plan.md §Performance ordering rule)
2. Validate name: trim(); if empty → throw ValidationError; reject /[\x00-\x1F\r\n]/ → throw ValidationError (red-team Pass 6); check length ≤ 100
3. Hash the token → tokenHash; SELECT id, email, role FROM invitation WHERE tokenHash=$1 (need the row to know email+role)
4. Begin transaction on the pool:
   a. Conditional UPDATE: UPDATE invitation SET status='accepted', "acceptedAt"=now() WHERE id=$1 AND status='pending' AND "expiresAt" > now() RETURNING id
   b. If 0 rows: rollback → throw InvalidLinkError (→ 410)
   c. Hash password: passwordHasher.hash(password) — Argon2id, same as seed/integration test
   d. INSERT INTO "user" (id, email, name, admin, emailVerified, createdAt, updatedAt) VALUES (randomUUID(), LOWER(invitation.email), trimmedName, role==='admin', false, now(), now())
   e. INSERT INTO account (id, userId, accountId, providerId, password, createdAt, updatedAt) VALUES (randomUUID(), userId, userId, 'credential', hashedPassword, now(), now())
   f. Catch 23505 on user insert: → rollback → throw AccountAlreadyExistsError (→ 409, plan.md Pass 2 race handling)
   g. Commit
5. Return { email: invitation.email }

## Export new error classes
- InvalidLinkError (→ 410 in controller)
- ValidationError (→ 400)
- AccountAlreadyExistsError (→ 409)

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationStore.test.ts --runInBand` passes (all tests GREEN)
- `yarn typecheck` passes

description:

**Skills**: /worker-request-handler
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts (EDIT — add anonymous routes + rate limiter)

## Task
Add the anonymous invitation endpoints and the invitationRateLimit middleware to invitationController.ts.

## Rate limiter middleware (invitationRateLimit)
- Table: invitationRateLimit (key text PK, count int, lastRequest bigint) — already migrated in Setup task
- Atomic UPSERT (plan.md Pass 3, data-model.md §Concurrency-safe increment):
  INSERT INTO "invitationRateLimit" (key, count, lastRequest) VALUES (key, 1, now_ms)
  ON CONFLICT (key) DO UPDATE SET
    count = CASE WHEN lastRequest < windowStart THEN 1 ELSE invitationRateLimit.count + 1 END,
    lastRequest = now_ms
  RETURNING count
  + pruning: DELETE FROM "invitationRateLimit" WHERE lastRequest < windowStart (same round-trip)
- If returned count > 10 → 429
- Enforce when: NODE_ENV \!== 'test' || process.env.BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_RATE_LIMIT === '1' (plan.md Pass 12 — reuse existing flag)
- Key = 'invitation:' + req.ip (honoring trust proxy=1)

## GET /api/auth/invitation/:token handler
- Apply rate limiter first
- Apply CSRF middleware (Origin allow-list — same shared helper as admin POST routes)
- Call lookupInvitation(pool, token)
- null → 410 with generic non-leaky message (FR-010)
- Result → res.setHeader('Cache-Control','no-store'); res.json({ email }) — NOT role (dropped in Pass 7)

## POST /api/auth/invitation/accept handler (CRITICAL: register BEFORE better-auth catch-all)
- Apply rate limiter first
- Apply CSRF middleware
- Route-scoped bodyParser.json({ limit: '4kb' }) with error handler mapping SyntaxError/PayloadTooLargeError → JSON 400 with generic message (Pass 8 — NOT HTML, NOT stack trace)
- Validate: token present, password 12..128, name 1..100 non-empty (pre-Argon2id validation)
- Call acceptInvitation(pool, token, password, name, cookieSecret)
- ValidationError → 400; InvalidLinkError → 410; AccountAlreadyExistsError → 409
- On success: res.setHeader('Cache-Control','no-store'); res.json({ email })

## serverApp.ts mounting order (CRITICAL — plan.md §Edge Cases)
- Anonymous /api/auth/invitation/* routes registered BEFORE app.all('/api/auth/*', toNodeHandler)
- Admin /api/admin/invitations routes are already after requireAdmin gate

title: US3 [GREEN]: Implement invitationStore list + retract + recopy paths
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-013..FR-019
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure, data-model.md §Management-list query, §State machine (retract conditional UPDATE), §tokenEnc encryption rules (re-copy), plan.md Pass 11 (retract TOCTOU fix, retract invitedByEmail JOIN)
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts (EDIT — add listInvitations, retractInvitation, getInvitationLink)

## Task
Add three new functions to invitationStore.ts to make the US3 RED tests pass.

## listInvitations(pool: Pool): Promise<InvitationSummary[]>
- Lazy-expire first: UPDATE invitation SET status='expired' WHERE status='pending' AND expiresAt <= now()
- SELECT invitation.id, email, role, status, createdAt, expiresAt, acceptedAt, user.email AS invitedByEmail
  FROM invitation
  INNER JOIN "user" ON "user".id = invitation."invitedBy"
  ORDER BY invitation."createdAt" DESC
- MUST NOT select tokenHash or tokenEnc (secrets never reach response)
- Return the array (empty if none exist — not an error)
- Set Cache-Control: no-store on the HTTP response (controller does this; store returns data)

## retractInvitation(pool: Pool, id: string): Promise<InvitationSummary>
- ATOMIC conditional UPDATE (plan.md Pass 11 — NOT a SELECT-then-UPDATE):
  UPDATE invitation SET status='retracted' WHERE id=$1 AND status='pending' RETURNING id
- If 0 rows updated:
  - SELECT 1 FROM invitation WHERE id=$1 → if no row: throw NotFoundError (→ 404)
  - if row exists: throw NotPendingError (→ 409) — invitation is not pending
- If 1 row: fetch and return the full InvitationSummary (with invitedByEmail via JOIN to user — same mapper as list)
- Set Cache-Control: no-store on the HTTP response (controller does this)

## getInvitationLink(pool: Pool, id: string, baseUrl: string, cookieSecret: string): Promise<{ link: string }>
- SELECT id, status, tokenEnc FROM invitation WHERE id=$1
- If not found: throw NotFoundError
- If status \!= 'pending': throw NotPendingError
- Decrypt: decryptToken(tokenEnc, cookieSecret) → plaintext token or null
- If null (decrypt failure after cookieSecret rotation): throw DecryptError (→ 409 'link unavailable', not 500)
- Return { link: buildInvitationLink(token, baseUrl) }

## New error classes to export
- NotFoundError (→ 404)
- NotPendingError (→ 409)
- DecryptError (→ 409 'link unavailable')

## Shared row-mapper
- Factor InvitationSummary assembly (invitation columns + user.email JOIN) into a shared private helper so list and retract cannot diverge (plan.md Pass 11 congruence)

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationStore.test.ts --runInBand` passes (all tests GREEN)
- `yarn typecheck` passes
close_reason: implemented listInvitations, retractInvitation, getInvitationLink in invitationStore.ts; fixed test call sites for getInvitationLink to pass baseUrl; typecheck passes

description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §SC-001..SC-009, §Edge Cases
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure (invitation.integration.test.ts), research.md §Cross-cutting: Testing strategy
**Skills**: /vitest-integration-testing

## Context
Integration tests run against the real compiled server (better-auth is ESM-only, hence the separate test harness). Use the existing invitation.integration.test.ts pattern established by auth.integration.test.ts.

## Why integration tests (in addition to unit tests)
Unit tests (invitationStore.test.ts, invitationController.test.ts) run under Jest/CommonJS and mock or stub better-auth-related behavior. The cross-layer behavior that requires a real running server:
- A newly created account via acceptInvitation() can actually sign in with better-auth (FR-012 end-to-end, plan.md Pass 8 latent coupling)
- Route ordering: anonymous invitation routes registered BEFORE the better-auth catch-all (plan.md §Edge Cases Route ordering)
- CSRF + rate-limit enforcement with BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_ORIGIN=1 + BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_RATE_LIMIT=1

## Scenarios to cover
1. Full create → redeem flow: admin creates invitation → recipient GETs token → recipient POSTs accept → account created with correct role → recipient signs in (FR-012 — better-auth sign-in on the created account works)
2. Single-use: reusing the accepted link → 410 (SC-003)
3. Retract: admin creates → admin retracts → recipient GETs token → 410 (link stopped working, SC-004)
4. Expiry: create an invitation with a past expiresAt (insert directly) → link returns 410
5. Rate limit: 11 rapid requests to GET /api/auth/invitation/:token → 429 (BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_RATE_LIMIT=1)
6. CSRF: POST /api/auth/invitation/accept with wrong Origin → 403 (BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_ORIGIN=1)
7. CSRF on admin POST: POST /api/admin/invitations with wrong Origin → 403
8. Body parsing: malformed JSON to POST /api/auth/invitation/accept → JSON 400 (not HTML)
9. Re-copy: admin creates → admin re-copies link → same link (FR-016)
10. Empty management list: GET /api/admin/invitations → [] (not error)
11. Log redaction: GET /invitation/<token> in production-like config → logger emits placeholder not raw token (unit-level test of the logger redaction helper, per plan.md Pass 7 note — or assert directly on the logger middleware unit)

## Acceptance
- `yarn test:integration` passes for the new invitation integration tests
- The full create → redeem → sign-in flow is exercised end-to-end

title: Final: Design quality pass — microcopy, empty-state, and non-leaky error messages
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-010, §SC-002
**Plan**: plan.md §Presentation Design §Quality Pass (/design-clarify for invalid-link and validation microcopy; /design-clarify for empty-state copy on management list)
**Skills**: /design-clarify
**Files**: Review all invitation UI components after implementation:
- src/frontend/web/invitations/CreateInvitation.tsx
- src/frontend/web/auth/RedeemInvitation.tsx
- src/frontend/web/invitations/InvitationsList.tsx
- src/core/i18n/locales/en.ts (string values)

## Quality pass goals (plan.md §Quality Pass)
**Design quality target**: MVP (internal admin tool; correctness and clarity over polish).
**What to review**:
1. Invalid-link error messages (RedeemInvitation): must be non-leaky (FR-010) — does not reveal which of unknown/accepted/retracted/expired applies; must be clear enough that the recipient knows what to do (contact the admin for a new invite)
2. Validation microcopy (CreateInvitation): error messages for malformed email, bad role, account-exists 409, active-pending 409 — are these clear and actionable for an admin?
3. Empty-state copy (InvitationsList): when no invitations exist, the message should orient the admin ('No invitations yet. Create one above.')
4. 429 rate-limit copy (RedeemInvitation): must clearly distinguish from the invalid-link terminal message — should invite a retry, not imply the link is dead (plan.md Pass 9)
5. Accessibility microcopy: copy-success announcement for the copy-link button must be announced clearly (not just 'Copied' but something descriptive like 'Invitation link copied to clipboard')

## What to do
- Review all string values in en.ts for the above criteria
- Refine any copy that is ambiguous, leaky, or fails the clarity bar
- Verify the 429 and 410 messages are DISTINCT from each other in both lookup and accept forms

## Acceptance
- All invalid-link messages are non-leaky (do not reveal which terminal state applies)
- 429 message is clearly transient and invites a retry
- Empty-state text is descriptive and action-oriented
- Copy-success live-region text is descriptive
- `yarn typecheck` still passes after any string-value changes

## Why Cypress E2E (in addition to unit + server integration tests)
The constitution (Principle I) requires E2E tests for all user-facing flows. The plan explicitly lists `cypress/integration/  # NEW — E2E for issue + redeem + manage flows`. The existing integration test task (9c7.5.6) covers server-side API flows (HTTP → DB) but does NOT exercise the React UI. Cypress exercises the real browser flow from the admin and recipient perspectives.

## Flows to cover
1. **US1 — Admin issues an invitation**: Sign in as admin, navigate to invitation management, fill create form (email + role), submit, verify link is shown and copy-link button works (visible text, clipboard API if testable)
2. **US2 — Recipient redeems an invitation**: Open the invitation link (/invitation/:token), verify email is pre-filled and locked (readonly), fill password + display name, submit, verify redirect to sign-in page
3. **US3 — Admin manages invitations**: Open management screen, verify all columns shown (email, role, status, created, accepted, created-by); retract a pending invitation → status shows Retracted; re-copy a pending link → same link shown; empty-state when no invitations
4. **Error flows**: Invalid/expired link shows non-leaky error (not the form); rejected creation (duplicate active invite) shows clear error

## Patterns to follow
- Mirror existing cypress/integration/login.spec.js for admin sign-in setup
- Mirror cypress/integration/adminLessons.spec.js for admin area navigation patterns
- Use the test DB reset endpoint (/api/test/reset-storage) for isolation if available, or use the admin API to create/retract invitations directly

- `yarn test-e2e` passes for the new invitation-system.spec.js
- All three user story flows exercised at the browser level

title: Remediate: invitationRateLimit prune does a full-table scan per request (no index on lastRequest)
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: migrations/1781767466145-AddInvitationRateLimitTable.js, src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts

**Finding** (MINOR / availability):
invitationRateLimit() runs `DELETE FROM "invitationRateLimit" WHERE "lastRequest" < $1` on every anonymous invitation request (invitationController.ts ~line 169), but the invitationRateLimit table has no index on "lastRequest" — only the "key" PRIMARY KEY. Each prune is therefore a sequential scan. The plan (plan.md lines 384-386) assumes this prune is "cheap, bounded" but did not add the supporting index. Combined with the already-accepted X-Forwarded-For spoofing risk (each distinct spoofed IP key inserts a row), an attacker who inflates the table turns every subsequent /api/auth/invitation/* request into a growing full-table scan — a mild DoS amplifier on the unauthenticated surface.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Add a btree index on "lastRequest" in the migration (or a follow-up migration):
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS "idx_invitationRateLimit_lastRequest" ON "invitationRateLimit"("lastRequest");
- Alternatively prune probabilistically (e.g. only ~1/N requests) so the scan cost is amortized, or prune only the current key's stale window.

**Acceptance**:
- A migration adds an index on invitationRateLimit("lastRequest") (or the prune no longer scans the whole table on every request).
- EXPLAIN on the prune DELETE uses the index (Index Scan, not Seq Scan) when the table has >1 row.
- Existing invitation rate-limit integration tests still pass.
close_reason: Added migration 1781767466146 with btree index on invitationRateLimit(lastRequest) to eliminate full-table scan during prune DELETE

title: Remediate: 409 error discrimination in invitationThunks never matches real server responses
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/web/invitations/invitationThunks.ts, src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts

**Finding**:
invitationThunks.ts lines 58-65 branch on 409 responses by checking `errorText.startsWith('account_exists')` and `errorText.startsWith('active_pending')`. The real server returns `err.message` which is `'An account already exists for <email>'` (AccountExistsError) and `'An active pending invitation already exists for <email>'` (ActivePendingError). Neither message starts with those prefixes, so every 409 falls through to the `'validation_error'` branch regardless of the actual cause. The unit test mocks the response body as `{ error: 'account_exists: ...' }` so it passes, but production responses never match.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Add a machine-readable discriminator to the 409 responses in invitationController.ts: return `{ error: err.message, code: err.code }` (err.code is 'ACCOUNT_EXISTS' or 'PENDING_INVITE_EXISTS').
- In invitationThunks.ts, branch on `body.code` rather than `errorText.startsWith(...)`.
- Update the unit test mocks to match the new server response shape.
- Verify with the integration test that the 409 body includes `code`.

**Acceptance**:
- POST /api/admin/invitations with an existing account email returns JSON `{ error: '...', code: 'ACCOUNT_EXISTS' }`.
- POST /api/admin/invitations for an already-pending email returns JSON `{ error: '...', code: 'PENDING_INVITE_EXISTS' }`.
- invitationThunks.ts branches correctly on the `code` field, not on message prefix.
- All existing invitationThunks unit tests updated and passing.
- Integration tests confirm the 409 body shape.
close_reason: Added code field to 409 responses in invitationController; invitationThunks now branches on body.code (ACCOUNT_EXISTS/PENDING_INVITE_EXISTS); tests updated to match real server shape

title: Remediate: invitation API routes absent from core/interfaces/Api.ts typed contract
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/core/interfaces/Api.ts, src/frontend/web/invitations/invitationThunks.ts, src/frontend/web/invitations/invitationsListThunks.ts, src/frontend/web/auth/redeemInvitationThunks.ts

**Finding**:
All invitation thunks (`invitationThunks.ts`, `invitationsListThunks.ts`, `redeemInvitationThunks.ts`) call the server via raw `fetch()` with untyped URL strings and manually-cast `response.json()`. Every other web-only route in this project (`/api/auth/get-session`, `/api/admin/languages`, etc.) is registered in `src/core/interfaces/Api.ts` with typed request params and response shapes, then accessed through the typed `RequestContext` API client. The invitation surface bypasses this contract layer, losing compile-time type checking on API call sites.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Add invitation route entries to the `APIGet` and `APIPost` interfaces in `src/core/interfaces/Api.ts` (under '// Web Only') with typed param and response shapes.
- Refactor the invitation thunks to use the typed RequestContext/useLoad API client, or at minimum import the response types from the core interface so the `as SomeType` casts are grounded in a shared definition.
- Note: the routes involve path params (`/api/auth/invitation/:token`, `/api/admin/invitations/:id/retract`) which may require new param types in the typed interface.

**Acceptance**:
- All invitation-related GET/POST routes appear in `src/core/interfaces/Api.ts`.
- `tsc --noEmit` across all projects still passes with no new errors.
- Invitation thunks no longer cast raw `response.json()` to ungrounded types.
close_reason: Work complete: invitation routes added to core Api.ts typed contract (6916cda95e06a33d95cc59c8beec2a2a451097a6), thunks grounded in shared types. Worker returned prose without br close; orchestrator closed after confirming HEAD advanced. [overnight autonomous recovery]
