title: Setup: user.deactivatedAt migration + test-isolation reset
description:
**Type**: CONFIGURATION
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/data-model.md §Migration, §Test-isolation delta (Decision 5)
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure (migrations/, jestSetupAfterEnv.ts)
**Reference**: mirrors migrations/1780760814404-AddBetterAuthTables.js style; jestSetupAfterEnv.ts already has an afterEach that deletes all users except the seeded admin — extend it, do not replace it.
**Files**:
- migrations/<Date.now()>-AddUserDeactivatedAt.js (CREATE NEW — timestamp strictly greater than the current last migration 1781767466147)
- src/server/jestSetupAfterEnv.ts (EDIT)

## Task
1. Write the migration: `up` runs `ALTER TABLE "user" ADD COLUMN "deactivatedAt" timestamptz` (nullable, no default). `down` runs `ALTER TABLE "user" DROP COLUMN "deactivatedAt"`. No index (roster scans ≤ tens of rows; enforcement lookups are by PK).
2. Extend jestSetupAfterEnv.ts's existing afterEach (after the existing session/user cleanup) to reset the spared rows to canonical state:
   ```sql
   UPDATE "user" SET "deactivatedAt" = NULL, admin = true
    WHERE LOWER(email) = $adminEmail OR id = $MOCK_USER_ID;
   ```
   so a test that deactivated or demoted the seeded admin / mock admin does not leak into the next test.

## Acceptance
- `yarn migrate:test` applies the migration cleanly; the down migration drops the column cleanly.
- `NODE_ENV=test npx jest --runInBand` still passes (no regressions from the afterEach change).
- The seeded admin (SeedAdminUser) has `deactivatedAt IS NULL` after migration (unaffected, stays Active).
close_reason: Added user.deactivatedAt migration (nullable, no index) and extended jestSetupAfterEnv afterEach to reset seeded admin/mock-admin deactivatedAt+admin fields; full suite (114/114, 1196 tests) and typecheck pass.

title: Final: Integration test — sign-in rejection, live-session revoke, concurrent last-admin guard (US2+US3)
description:
**Type**: TDD (integration)
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/spec.md §SC-003..SC-005, §Edge Cases ("Last-admin guardrail under concurrency")
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure (src/server/auth/userAccounts.integration.test.ts NEW), quickstart.md §Test entry points, data-model.md §Last-admin lock
**Reference**: mirrors src/server/auth/invitation.integration.test.ts and auth.integration.test.ts (real compiled server + real better-auth child process — better-auth is ESM-only, hence the separate integration harness; NOT the Jest/CommonJS unit mock)
**Files**: src/server/auth/userAccounts.integration.test.ts (CREATE NEW)

## Why integration tests (in addition to the unit tests already written per-story)
Unit tests (userStore.test.ts, usersController.test.ts, requireUser.test.ts) run under Jest/CommonJS with the better-auth CJS mock, which caches `session.user` at sign-in and does NOT observe a mid-session deactivation or role change. The cross-layer behaviors below require the real compiled server + real better-auth.

## Scenarios to cover
1. Full deactivate flow end-to-end: admin signs in (real better-auth), a second account is deactivated via the API, that account's live session's next request is unauthenticated, AND a fresh sign-in attempt with correct credentials fails (both enforcement points, real server)
2. Reactivate restores sign-in: deactivate -> reactivate -> the account can sign in again with its original credentials
3. Concurrent last-admin guard, deactivate vs deactivate: with exactly two active admins, fire two concurrent `POST .../deactivate` requests each targeting the OTHER admin — assert exactly one succeeds (200) and one is refused (409 LAST_ADMIN), never both
4. Concurrent last-admin guard, demote vs demote: same shape as #3 but via `POST .../role {role:'standard'}`
5. Concurrent last-admin guard, CROSS mutation type: with exactly two active admins, fire one deactivate and one demote concurrently, each targeting the OTHER admin — assert the system never reaches zero active admins (at least one of the two is refused)
6. Role-change propagation without revoke: promote a Standard user via the API; without any explicit session refresh/revoke, their very next authenticated request reflects the new admin role (Decision 2 — cookieCache disabled)
7. Same-origin (CSRF) enforcement on all four mutating POST routes (role, deactivate, reactivate, revoke-sessions) — wrong Origin -> 403, mirrors the existing invitation controller's requireSameOrigin integration coverage

## Acceptance
`yarn test:integration` passes for the new userAccounts.integration.test.ts, including the concurrency scenarios (3-5) which are the load-bearing regression guard for FR-012.

title: Final: Cypress E2E — user management roster + role/deactivate/reactivate/force-sign-out flows
description:
**Type**: TDD (E2E)
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/spec.md §US1..US4 + Acceptance Scenarios
**Plan**: plan.md §Constitution Check §I (E2E for user-facing flows required by Principle I)
**Reference**: mirrors cypress/integration/login.spec.js for admin sign-in setup and the existing invitation-system Cypress spec (cypress/integration/invitation-system.spec.js, if present) for admin-area navigation and management-list patterns
**Files**: cypress/integration/user-account-management.spec.js (CREATE NEW)

## Flows to cover
1. **US1 — Roster**: sign in as admin, navigate to Users (nav link beside Invitations), verify all columns shown (name, email, role, status, created), own row marked "You"
2. **US2 — Deactivate/Reactivate**: deactivate a second account (two-step confirm), verify it shows Deactivated; sign in as that user in a second session/tab and verify sign-in is refused; reactivate; verify that user can sign in again
3. **US2 guardrails**: attempt to deactivate own account -> disabled/refused visibly; attempt to deactivate the sole other admin after demoting all but one -> refused with visible reason
4. **US3 — Promote/Demote**: promote a Standard user (no confirm needed), verify Admin badge; demote back (two-step confirm), verify Standard again; attempt to demote the last admin -> disabled/refused visibly
5. **US4 — Force sign-out**: force-sign-out a user with an active session (two-step confirm), verify the account stays Active and the target's session ends; sign back in as that user successfully
6. **Access control**: a signed-in Standard user hitting `/admin/users` is blocked from the screen (no roster data visible)

## Acceptance
`yarn test-e2e` passes for the new user-account-management.spec.js, covering all four user stories at the browser level.

title: US2: Deactivate and reactivate account access
description:
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/spec.md §User Story 2 - Deactivate and reactivate account access (Priority: P1)
**Goal**: An administrator deactivates a departing/compromised account (blocks sign-in, revokes active sessions immediately) and can reactivate it later; the last remaining Admin and the acting admin's own account cannot be deactivated (FR-005..FR-008, FR-012).
**Acceptance**: Deactivate an account with an active session -> session stops being authenticated on next request AND sign-in is refused; reactivate -> sign-in works again; attempt to deactivate the last Admin -> refused; attempt to deactivate own account -> refused; both guardrails hold under concurrency (spec §US2 Acceptance Scenarios 1-5, Edge Cases).

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_

## Security note (carried from plan.md §Security Considerations)
Both enforcement points (sign-in hook + session-load check) MUST fail CLOSED on error, never open. This is tested explicitly in US2.3/US2.4 below — do not skip the injected-error-branch tests.

title: US2 [RED]: Write failing unit tests for userStore.deactivateAccount + reactivateAccount
description:
**Type**: RED
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/spec.md §US2, §FR-005..FR-008, §FR-012, §Edge Cases (last-admin under concurrency, self-action, already-deactivated no-op, no-active-sessions no-op)
**Plan**: data-model.md §Store operations (deactivateAccount, reactivateAccount), §Last-admin lock (shared fragment), §State model
**Reference**: mirrors src/server/auth/invitationStore.test.ts's transactional-mutation test style (real test DB, no pg mocking); the FOR UPDATE lock pattern is new to this feature — test it by opening two concurrent client connections against getAuthPool() and asserting only one of two concurrent deactivate-the-second-to-last-admin calls succeeds.
**Files**: src/server/auth/userStore.test.ts (EDIT — add deactivateAccount/reactivateAccount describe blocks)

## Test cases to cover
1. `deactivateAccount(pool, actingUserId, targetId)`: sets `deactivatedAt = now()` and DELETEs the target's sessions in one transaction, on a Standard user target
2. Self-lockout: `targetId === actingUserId` -> throws `SelfDeactivationError`, no change made
3. Last-admin: target is the only active admin -> throws `LastAdminError`, no change made
4. Idempotent no-op: deactivating an already-deactivated account returns the current (Deactivated) row without error
5. Target not found -> throws `UserNotFoundError`
6. `reactivateAccount(pool, targetId)`: sets `deactivatedAt = NULL`
7. Idempotent no-op: reactivating an already-active account returns the current (Active) row without error
8. Reactivate on not-found target -> throws `UserNotFoundError`
9. Deactivate a user with zero active sessions succeeds without error (nothing to revoke)
10. Concurrency: with two active admins A and B, two concurrent `deactivateAccount` calls each targeting the OTHER admin must not both succeed — at least one is refused with `LastAdminError` (spec Edge Case — never reach zero admins)

## Acceptance
`npx jest src/server/auth/userStore.test.ts --runInBand` fails on the new deactivate/reactivate tests (RED state; listAccounts tests from US1 still pass).

title: US2 [GREEN]: Implement userStore.deactivateAccount + reactivateAccount
description:
**Type**: GREEN
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/spec.md §US2, §FR-005..FR-008, §FR-012
**Plan**: data-model.md §Store operations, §Last-admin lock (shared fragment — Decision 4), research.md Decision 4
**Reference**: mirrors src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts's atomic conditional-UPDATE discipline (retractInvitation), extended with explicit row locking per data-model.md
**Files**: src/server/auth/userStore.ts (EDIT)

## Task
Implement per data-model.md §Store operations exactly:
- `deactivateAccount(pool, actingUserId, targetId)`: if `targetId === actingUserId` -> `SelfDeactivationError` (checked BEFORE opening the transaction). Else, in a transaction: `SELECT id FROM "user" WHERE admin = true AND "deactivatedAt" IS NULL ORDER BY id FOR UPDATE` (lock active-admin set); fetch target (else `UserNotFoundError`); if already deactivated -> COMMIT and return current row (no-op); if target is an active admin and locked-set count <= 1 -> ROLLBACK, `LastAdminError`; else `UPDATE "user" SET "deactivatedAt" = now(), "updatedAt" = now() WHERE id = $1 AND "deactivatedAt" IS NULL` then `DELETE FROM "session" WHERE "userId" = $1`; COMMIT; return updated `AccountSummary`.
- `reactivateAccount(pool, targetId)`: fetch target (else `UserNotFoundError`); `UPDATE "user" SET "deactivatedAt" = NULL, "updatedAt" = now() WHERE id = $1` (no-op if already active); return updated `AccountSummary`.

## Acceptance
`npx jest src/server/auth/userStore.test.ts --runInBand` passes (including the concurrency test). `yarn typecheck` passes.

title: US2 [GREEN]: Implement auth.ts sign-in-rejection hook + requireUser.ts session-load check (both fail-closed)
description:
**Type**: GREEN
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/spec.md §US2, §FR-005
**Plan**: data-model.md §Enforcement points; plan.md §Security Considerations "Fail-closed enforcement of deactivation (High)"
**Reference**: mirrors the existing databaseHooks usage pattern already present in auth.ts (if any) or better-auth's `databaseHooks.session.create.before` API; mirrors requireUser.ts's existing loadSession() structure
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.ts (EDIT), src/server/middle/requireUser.ts (EDIT)

## Task
- `auth.ts`: add `databaseHooks.session.create.before` — `SELECT "deactivatedAt" FROM "user" WHERE id = session.userId`; if non-NULL, `throw new APIError("UNAUTHORIZED", ...)` so no session row is minted. **Fail closed**: do NOT wrap the query in try/catch-and-continue — if the query itself throws (pool/DB error), let the error propagate so sign-in fails rather than silently succeeding.
- `requireUser.ts` `loadSession()`: after `getSession()` returns a session, `SELECT "deactivatedAt" FROM "user" WHERE id = $1`; if non-NULL OR the query itself fails for any reason, return `null` / leave `req.user` unset (treated unauthenticated). **Fail closed**: any failure path here must resolve to "unauthenticated", never re-throw into a 500 that could be misread, and never silently continue as authenticated.

## Acceptance
`npx jest src/server/middle/requireUser.test.ts --runInBand` passes. `yarn test:integration` passes for auth.integration.test.ts. `yarn typecheck` passes. Both fail-closed branches (US2.3 items 3 and the integration equivalent) are green.

title: US3: Change a user's role
description:
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/spec.md §User Story 3 - Change a user's role (Priority: P2)
**Goal**: An administrator promotes a Standard user to Admin or demotes an Admin to Standard; the system refuses to demote the last remaining Admin (FR-003, FR-004, FR-013).
**Acceptance**: Promote a Standard user -> gains administrative access; demote -> access removed; attempt to demote the last Admin -> refused (spec §US3 Acceptance Scenarios 1-4).

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_

## Design note (research.md Decision 2)
No session revocation on role change — cookieCache is disabled by default, so loadSession() re-reads user.admin fresh every request; a role change takes effect on the target's very next request automatically. Do not add a revoke step.

## Red-team note (plan.md §Edge Cases & Error Handling — "Self-demotion evicts the acting admin")
Self-demotion is permitted while another active admin remains, and takes effect on the NEXT request — so the admin's own next `/api/admin/*` call 403s immediately after self-demoting. The frontend task below (US3.5) MUST implement the redirect-on-self-demotion mitigation; do not treat this as a server bug.

4. `{role: 'bogus'}` or missing `role` -> 400 `{code: 'INVALID_ROLE'}`
5. Nonexistent id -> 404 `{code: 'USER_NOT_FOUND'}`
6. Missing/failed same-origin header -> 403
7. Unauthenticated -> 401; non-admin -> 403
8. An admin demoting THEMSELVES (with another admin present) -> 200, `role: 'standard'`, `isSelf: true` on the response row

## Acceptance
`npx jest src/server/controllers/usersController.test.ts --runInBand` fails on the new role-route tests (RED state; US1/US2 controller tests still pass).

title: US4: Force sign-out without deactivating
description:
**Spec**: specs/006-user-account-management/spec.md §User Story 4 - Force sign-out without deactivating (Priority: P3)
**Goal**: An administrator revokes a user's active sessions without deactivating the account — the user is forced to sign in again, but the account stays Active (FR-009).
**Acceptance**: Force-sign-out a user with an active session -> session ends immediately, account remains Active, user can sign back in (spec §US4 Acceptance Scenarios 1-3).

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_

## Red-team note (plan.md §Edge Cases & Error Handling — "Force-sign-out on one's own row evicts the acting admin (Low)")
`revokeSessions` deletes ALL of the target's sessions with no self-exclusion, and force-sign-out is intentionally NOT disabled on the self row (it's a legitimate "sign out all my devices" action). The frontend task below (US4.5) MUST implement the self-targeted warning + graceful redirect mitigation — this is by design, not a bug to "fix" by disabling the self row.
